summary: Matched HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags in src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.c, removed its assembly stub from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s, and added pobj.o to the EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED build list. The match required correcting PObj-related header types: HSD_PObjGetFlags now returns u32, the HSD_PObj union gained an x14_unk arm, and the previous direct aobj field was replaced by an inferred struct at union offset 0x14 containing an HSD_AObj pointer at offset 0x20. The PR body notes that the structure seemed different from what the author was used to; there was no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Removed the hand-written assembly listing for HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.s.
Implemented HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags in C as a null-guarded traversal over a linked HSD_PObj list, calling HSD_PObjRemoveAnimByFlags on each node.

Added inline HSD_PObjRemoveAnimByFlags, which checks for a non-null PObj, tests flags & POBJ_ANIM, confirms pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM, removes the associated HSD_AObj, and clears the aobj pointer.
Changed HSD_PObjGetFlags return type from u16 to u32 to match the PObj flags field usage.
Introduced struct _unk_struct_pobj with data[8] followed by struct _HSD_AObj* aobj at offset 0x20.
Changed HSD_PObj's union at offset 0x14 to include struct _unk_struct_pobj* x14_unk and changed envelope_list to use struct _HSD_SList*.
Removed the direct struct _HSD_AObj* aobj field from HSD_PObj, reflecting that the animation object pointer is reached through the offset-0x14 union payload for shape animation PObjs.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/pobj.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
smart_moves: Modeled the observed assembly access pattern directly: the assembly loads pobj + 0x14, then loads and clears offset 0x20 from that pointee, so the C header introduced an intermediate unknown struct rather than forcing a direct HSD_PObj::aobj field.
Kept the helper HSD_PObjRemoveAnimByFlags inline, which likely helped reproduce the original control flow and avoid introducing an extra function call separate from the matched target.
Preserved the assembly's null behavior with an early return in both the all-list function and the per-PObj helper.

Encoded the bit-test behavior as flags & POBJ_ANIM and the PObj type check as pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM, making the matched assembly more readable while staying close to the original condition sequence.
Cleared the animation pointer after calling HSD_AObjRemove, matching the assembly store of zero after the removal call.
decomp_lessons: When matching baselib object structs, do not assume a visible pointer field belongs directly to the parent struct; assembly may show that a union member points to a secondary layout containing the field.
For PObj shape animation, the animation object pointer was accessed through pobj->u.x14_unk->aobj rather than through a direct HSD_PObj::aobj field in this slice.
Return types for flag accessors should match the width of the underlying flags field and generated code; here HSD_PObjGetFlags needed u32 rather than u16.
A small helper can be made inline to express repeated logic without changing the generated call structure for the matched function.
After removing a function from an asm file, remember to add the corresponding object to the appropriate build list so the C implementation is built.
naming_conventions: Used HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags for the public matched function.
Used HSD_PObjRemoveAnimByFlags for the inline per-object helper.
Used x14_unk to name an unknown union member at offset 0x14, preserving offset information in the name.
Used _unk_struct_pobj / unk_struct as placeholder names for an inferred but not fully understood PObj-related layout.
Retained HSD-style type names and casts such as HSD_PObj, HSD_AObj, HSD_POBJ, HSD_POBJ_INFO, and HSD_POBJ_METHOD.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the top-level cmplwi r3,0 / beq into an early null return in HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags.
Matched the linked-list traversal by initializing a local pointer to pobj and advancing through pp = pp->next until NULL.
Matched the flags mask instruction sequence by storing flags & POBJ_ANIM behavior before conditionally removing shape animation.
Matched the type check against the PObj type bits by using pobj_type(pobj) == POBJ_SHAPEANIM.
Matched the load from offset 0x14 followed by load/store at offset 0x20 by adding an inferred nested struct with aobj at 0x20.
Matched the post-removal zero store by assigning unk_struct->aobj = NULL after HSD_AObjRemove.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text noted: "Structure seems to be different from what I'm used to."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename _unk_struct_pobj or x14_unk to a known baselib type.
Search for other PObj functions that access pobj->u.x14_unk->aobj or the offset 0x14 union.
Check whether HSD_PObjRemoveAnimByFlags remains inline or becomes a public symbol in later decomp work.
Look for later corrections to HSD_PObj struct layout, especially around shape_set, envelope_list, and shape animation payloads.
Search for additional EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED additions for sysdolphin/baselib object files.
